reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Monday 20 July
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Tuesday 14 July to Sunday 19 July.
reports.summary: The week from Tuesday 14 July to Sunday 19 July averaged about 23.7 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 42.8% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Sunday 19 July, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 55.9% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Thursday 16 July and lowest on Saturday 18 July.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Sunday 19 July, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 55.9% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 7.0 GW average output on Saturday 18 July.
Solar was strongest on Thursday 16 July, averaging about 5.0 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 7.4 GW across the period, rising highest on Thursday 16 July and falling lowest on Saturday 18 July.
Measured generation was highest on Thursday 16 July, at roughly 26.9 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Sunday 19 July
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Sunday 19 July had the strongest renewable share at 55.9%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Saturday 18 July
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 2.9 GW on Saturday 18 July. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Thursday 16 July
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 11.6 GW on Thursday 16 July. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 7.0 GW on Saturday 18 July.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 5.0 GW on Thursday 16 July.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 11.6 GW on Thursday 16 July; lowest was about 2.9 GW on Saturday 18 July.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Sunday 19 July
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Sunday 19 July, measured average generation was about 19.3 GW. Renewables averaged 55.9% of measured generation, gas averaged 3.0 GW, wind averaged 5.9 GW and solar averaged 4.6 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 55.9% average renewable share on Sunday 19 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 7.0 GW average wind output on Saturday 18 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 5.0 GW average solar output on Thursday 16 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 11.6 GW average gas output on Thursday 16 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 55.9% on Sunday 19 July
• Highest average wind output: ~7.0 GW on Saturday 18 July
• Highest average solar output: ~5.0 GW on Thursday 16 July
• Highest average gas output: ~11.6 GW on Thursday 16 July

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-07-20/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
